title: The BrightPath Outage: Students Locked Out

narrator.personality: Junior SRE on their first solo on-call shift. Nervous but thorough. Keeps mentioning that students have midterm deadlines tomorrow.

story_beats.message: Support inbox just hit 40 tickets. A professor tweeted that BrightPath is down during midterm week. The CEO is asking for a status update.
Engineering lead just joined the call. She says the last deployment was a security hardening sprint -- they tightened firewall rules across all environments.
That is not matching what we see. The instance itself is running fine -- this looks like a network access issue. Check what traffic is allowed to reach the instance.

hints: The application is running on the instance. Can you check what network rules control access to it?
Security groups act as virtual firewalls for EC2 instances. What inbound rules are configured?
Look at the inbound rules -- is port 443 allowed? The application serves HTTPS traffic on that port.
The security group has no inbound rule for port 443. That means all HTTPS traffic from users is being dropped at the network level.

resolution.root_cause: The EC2 instance security group sg-0a1b2c3d4e5f67890 was modified during a security hardening sprint, removing the inbound rule for port 443 (HTTPS). All user traffic to the BrightPath learning platform is dropped at the security group level.

fix_criteria.description: Player identifies that the security group is missing an inbound rule for port 443 (HTTPS)
Player connects the missing rule to the recent security hardening deployment
Player proposes adding an inbound rule allowing TCP port 443 from 0.0.0.0/0 (or appropriate CIDR) to the security group

learning_objectives: Security groups are stateful firewalls that control inbound and outbound traffic to EC2 instances
Security group rules are deny-by-default -- if no rule explicitly allows traffic, it is dropped
Changes to security groups take effect immediately with no downtime or restart required
